specific_location: On fenceline that runs from red barn west of Holmes residence, west to The Casita at Chico Basin Ranch.
description: This bird has the two crisp, unworn buffy wingbars of a hatch year bird. This bird is still mostly in juvenal plumage and will retain this plumage until October. (Pyle)  Head slightly crested and somewhat grayer than the back.  Small round whitish eyering. Long and large wide bill with orange undersides to mandible. Throat immaculate white, strongly contrasting with sides of head and breast. Breast not visible in any photos but appeared dull creamy white in person. Long rictal bristles. Rather short primaries and tertials strongly edged yellow, fresh plumage. Tail relatively long and dark gray. Wings appear quite short, slightly longer than the base of the tail.  Moderate primary projection. Back dark brown with greenish tones on upper back.Behaviors: Foraged quite low in giant ragweed, Embrosia trifida. Perched for long periods of time on upper and lower strand of barbed wire fence near ragweed and giant sunflower. At times could hear bill snap.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenal
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
